stories.headline: Pound, Gilts Rise After UK Prime Minister Starmer Resigns
stories.summary: The pound and gilts edged higher after UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigned and the path to power began to clear for the frontrunner to succeed him.

stories.headline: Alan Greenspan, Fed Chairman Through Prosperity and Crisis, Dies at 100
stories.summary: The pre-eminent economic policymaker of his time and a skilled political operator, he favored market-friendly stances that would later come to be associated with destructive financial forces.

stories.headline: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation plan
stories.summary: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer was visibly emotional as he announced plans to step down.

stories.headline: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer announces resignation
stories.summary: Starmer's main rival Andy Burnham says he will stand in any contest to replace outgoing Labour Party leader.

stories.headline: Political turmoil: UK will see its seventh prime minister in 10 years
stories.summary: After less than two years as PM, Keir Starmer announces his resignation amid mounting pressure from own Labour Party.

stories.headline: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer’s resignation speech in full
stories.summary: The UK is to get its seventh prime minister in less than 10 years, after Keir Starmer confirmed he is to step down.

stories.headline: UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer resigns as Andy Burnham announces candidacy
stories.summary: Starmer will continue as caretaker prime minister until a new Labour leader is elected, while Reform UK has demanded a general election.

stories.headline: Keir Starmer steps down as UK prime minister
stories.summary: Departure comes as Wes Streeting backs Andy Burnham to be the next PM

stories.headline: Alan Greenspan dies aged 100
stories.summary: Longstanding Federal Reserve chair was lionised in office — but his legacy was reassessed in the financial crisis

stories.headline: UK Labour Party Leadership contest may loom after UK PM Starmer resigns
stories.summary: UK Labour Party Leadership contest may loom after UK PM Starmer resigns.

stories.headline: First round of US-Iran talks ends with encouraging progress, mediators say
stories.summary: The US-Iran talks began on Sunday in Switzerland, after last week's agreement, which includes a commitment to reach a final deal within 60 days.

stories.headline: Alan Greenspan, architect of the modern American economy, dies aged 100
stories.summary: As chairman of the Federal Reserve, Alan Greenspan became the world's most high-profile banker.

stories.headline: Inside Ukraine’s campaign to turn Russian-occupied Crimea into an 'island'
stories.summary: No petrol sales for civilians, a rolling power-outage schedule and a cancelled tourism season: what is happening in Russia-annexed Crimea amid Kyiv’s campaign to cut off the peninsula from Moscow’s control?

stories.headline: US-backed political newcomer wins Colombia election, marking country's right-wing shift
stories.summary: With nearly all votes counted, the election results polarised the country, sparking both celebrations and protests across the country.

stories.headline: Trump-backed political outsider wins Colombia election, initial count shows
stories.summary: Abelardo de la Espriella appears to have narrowly defeated his rival Iván Cepeda, who says the preliminary count is "not yet official or binding".

stories.headline: Schools close as more than half of France under red heat alerts
stories.summary: The health minister warns many citizens "will suffer", with temperatures set to peak on Monday.

stories.headline: Over 400 ships are waiting near Hormuz for the strait to reopen fully
stories.summary: Many tanker operators reluctant to move vessels until Iran and the US strengthen ceasefire deal

stories.headline: Embattled Superintendent of Los Angeles School District Resigns
stories.summary: Alberto Carvalho resigned Sunday, months after the F.B.I. raided his home and office. He came to Los Angeles from Miami with a reputation for raising student test scores but also made missteps.

stories.headline: Pakistan issues nationwide alert over fears of heavy rains, floods
stories.summary: Warning comes as country braces for likely fourth consecutive year of punishing monsoon, expected to arrive this month.

stories.headline: Far-right de la Espriella elected Colombia president: What’s next?
stories.summary: Election results show Trump-endorsed millionaire Abelardo de la Espriella wins by razor-thin margin.

stories.headline: Cape Verde produce another World Cup shock to keep remarkable run alive
stories.summary: Cape Verde supporters in the United States celebrated after their team scored its first-ever World Cup goals and earned a 2-2 draw against Uruguay.

stories.headline: China's import of custard apples is sparking fears in Taiwan
stories.summary: Taiwan's agriculture ministry is worried that Beijing wants to weaponise a local specialty: the atemoya.

stories.headline: Protest led by India's 'cockroach party' enters its third day
stories.summary: The group has said the protest will continue until Education Minister Dharmendra Pradhan steps down.

stories.headline: 'I witnessed Maradona's Hand of God' - a goal still talked about 40 years on
stories.summary: One BBC journalist's first football game had one of the sport's most controversial moments and also one of the most celebrated.

stories.headline: Mexico Is Tapping Global Debt Markets to Fund Bond Buyback
stories.summary: Mexico is tapping global bond markets to fund a buyback of its existing debt as pressure mounts from credit-rating agencies to reign in the government’s deficit.

stories.headline: RBC Says Stocks Can Weather Fed Hikes, But Not Too Many
stories.summary: Lori Calvasina, head of US equity strategy research at RBC Capital Markets, says the strength of US corporate earnings is propping up stocks amid concern that the Federal Reserve will raise interest rates. But there are limits to how many hikes the market can withstand, she says on Bloomberg Television's ``Surveillance.'' (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Buys $34.9 Million of Bitcoin Using Common Stock
stories.summary: Michael Saylor’s Strategy Inc. bought $39.4 million of Bitcoin, leaning again on its common stock to fund purchases of the cryptocurrency for a third consecutive week despite earlier pledges to pivot to perpetual preferred shares.

stories.headline: CRH to Buy Arcosa in Building Materials Firm’s Biggest Deal
stories.summary: CRH Plc agreed to acquire US construction services company Arcosa for $8.5 billion in what is a record deal for the Irish building materials provider.

stories.headline: Cuomo to Lead Crypto Venture With New York Stock Exchange Owner
stories.summary: Former New York Governor Andrew Cuomo is starting a second act: Helping cryptocurrency exchange operator OKX and the owner of the New York Stock Exchange build a new business that merges digital and traditional finance markets.

stories.headline: Saudi Central Bank Said to Pull Money From at Least Two Managers
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia’s monetary authority has pulled billions of dollars from at least two global asset managers in recent months, in a sign that the custodian of one of the world’s deepest pools of capital is growing more selective with deployment.

stories.headline: Colombia Bonds Rise as Market Favorite Espriella Wins Vote
stories.summary: Colombian bonds rose after Abelardo de la Espriella won one of the tightest presidential elections in the nation’s history, setting the stage for a swing to market-friendly policies that have sparked rallies across Latin America.

stories.headline: Uzbekistan Seeks More Western Gas Investors After BP Deal
stories.summary: Uzbekistan is stepping up efforts to bring major European and US energy companies into its natural gas sector, after last month securing investment from BP Plc.

stories.headline: Portugal Eyes Wealth Fund for Strategic Stakes, Premier Says
stories.summary: The Portuguese government plans to create a sovereign wealth fund that would allow the state to retain stakes in strategic sectors of the economy.

stories.headline: UK Bonds Welcome Lower Odds of Prolonged Leadership Battle
The Dispute Over Events for America’s 250th Birthday, Explained
stories.summary: Two national groups, including one backed by President Trump, have planned an array of activities to celebrate the country’s semiquincentennial. States are also hosting many events.

stories.summary: Fuel had already been rationed due to shortages caused by Kyiv's attacks against supply routes in Russian-occupied territories.

stories.headline: The BTS fans losing thousands as scammers cash in on comeback tour ticket war
stories.summary: As ticket demand outstrips supply by a ratio of 15 to one, desperate fans have become easy targets.

stories.headline: Boyle Heights Warehouse Fire Spreads Smoke Across Los Angeles
stories.summary: Firefighters say they are making progress against the blaze, which began Wednesday at a cold-storage facility. But smoke continued to blow across the region, and the authorities reiterated air-quality warnings.

stories.headline: How Trump’s Fragile Agreement With Iran Is Shaping the Midterms
stories.summary: Democrats say the president started an economically painful war that resulted in nothing positive. Republicans are more divided, even as they show some signs of relief at falling gas prices.

stories.headline: Currency crash and visa crackdowns force Indian students to rethink studying abroad
stories.summary: A weaker rupee and tougher immigration rules are pushing Indian students away from popular destinations like the US and UK.

stories.headline: Stocks Drift Despite Drop in Oil on US-Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
stories.summary: US stocks are heading for a quiet open after the long weekend, with equities finding little support from lower oil prices amid signs of diplomatic headway between the US and Iran.

stories.headline: For cash-strapped farmers, deal to end Iran fighting comes too late
stories.summary: Some of the president’s strongest supporters are hurting as midterm elections approach.

stories.headline: Zelensky returns highest Polish honour after award stripped
stories.summary: Ukraine's president said his country was open to "engagement" about "difficult and painful chapters of our shared past".

stories.headline: Israeli strikes kill six people in Gaza including Al Jazeera cameraman, officials say
stories.summary: The Israeli military accused Ahmed Wishah of being a "Hamas sniper operative", without providing evidence.

stories.headline: Lebanese turtle conservationist Mona Khalil killed by Israeli strike
stories.summary: Mona Khalil, who had refused to leave the beach she had spent years protecting, died after several days in hospital, an environmental group said.

stories.headline: Bolivian president declares state of emergency
stories.summary: The move comes after weeks of anti-government protests that have caused a shortage of basic goods in Bolivia.

stories.headline: Israel and Hezbollah continue strikes despite ceasefire agreement
stories.summary: At least 20 people have reportedly been killed by Israeli air strikes as the Israeli military said Hezbollah had fired at Israeli troops in southern Lebanon.

stories.headline: African and Caribbean nations call for formal apology for transatlantic slavery
stories.summary: The leaders have asked for apologies from the countries that benefited from the slave trade, as well as debt relief and financial compensation.

stories.headline: Meloni tells Trump to 'focus on your own popularity' as row escalates
stories.summary: The US president earlier questioned Meloni's popularity after suggesting she "begged" for a photo at G7 summit

stories.summary: Experts say internet, media and government restrictions have all hampered casualty reporting across the region.

stories.headline: US-Iran talks postponed as Israel launches deadly strikes in Lebanon
stories.summary: JD Vance has not travelled to Switzerland as the Israeli military said it was targeting Hezbollah and that four of its own soldiers had been killed.

stories.headline: Meet Merlin the Duck: Mexico's World Cup 'ambassador'
stories.summary: The BBC’s Will Grant was amongst the crowds swarming to meet Merlin, a pet duck who went viral for waddling around Mexico City in the Mexican team's jersey.

stories.headline: Bowen: US-Iran deal raises inescapable question of what the war was for
stories.summary: While the human cost is clear, the Iranian regime has not just survived the war, it has been empowered.

stories.headline: US-Iran deal leaves core sticking points unresolved - and a $300bn question
stories.summary: Trump has insisted the deal ensures that Iran will never buy, develop or produce a nuclear weapon. But text of the agreement falls short of that.

stories.headline: What Iran and US get from deal and why both could struggle to keep it
stories.summary: BBC analysts assess the claims by both Washington and Tehran to have won a victory with the deal to end their war.

stories.headline: Suspected gang leader shot dead in flower bouquet ambush at airport
stories.summary: The gunmen concealed their weapons behind flowers and stuffed toys while waiting for the victim outside Guayaquil airport.

stories.headline: Bolivia signs $20m deal with US to fight drug trafficking
stories.summary: Under its new president, Bolivia is re-establishing severed ties with the US to combat organised crime.

stories.summary: The retired couple tell BBC Newsnight they tried to show the warship they had changed course in the English Channel before the shots were fired.

stories.headline: Tehran selling deal with US as victory – but for Iranians it was necessity
stories.summary: For many Iranians, the question is not whether the deal means victory, but whether it lowers prices and reduces fear of another war.

stories.headline: Bowen: Iran deal ends Trump's war that revealed limit of US dominance
stories.summary: The deal to end fighting and re-open the Strait of Hormuz leaves the sides where they were 24 hours before the war - only with thousands now dead.

stories.headline: Dublin-born Cape Verde star recruited on LinkedIn gets World Cup chance
stories.summary: They are one of the smallest countries to play in a World Cup, but Cape Verde are aiming high, with a Dublin-born defender who used to work in a bank among their ranks.

stories.headline: What one country's experiment says about attempts to boost birth rates
stories.summary: Why did Hungary’s pronatalist approach deliver an early rise in births only then to fall back? And what lessons does it offer to other countries desperate to lift fertility?
